subject: Databases
title: SQL Injection Explained
description: Learn SQL Injection Explained in one minute.

hook.text: A hacker typed ' OR 1=1 -- into a login form and got access to every account. SQL injection at work.

buildup.text: If the app builds SQL by gluing strings, user input becomes part of the query. That's the vulnerability.

discovery.text: Parameterized queries separate data from SQL: WHERE email = $1. The input is never treated as code.

twist.text: SQL injection is decades old but still in the OWASP Top 10. It's preventable yet still widespread.

climax.text: ORMs and query builders use parameters by default. Raw string queries are the main risk.

punchline.text: Never glue user input into SQL. Use parameters. Always.

quiz.question: How do parameterized queries prevent SQL injection?
options: They treat user input as data, never as executable SQL code
They encrypt the SQL before sending it
They block all queries that contain special characters
